channel.title: Failure Build — Changelog
channel.description: New features and bug fixes in Failure Build, the terminal coding agent forked from xAI's Grok Build.

item.title: New: /afk mode — agent decides what to do while you are away
item.description: /afk turns on unattended coding: always-approve plus an autonomous goal so the agent keeps picking and shipping the next high-value change. Optional focus text steers the work (/afk polish the README). /afk off pauses and restores permission prompts; the status chip labels AFK goals distinctly.

item.title: New: provider presets + first-launch setup wizard
item.description: Built-in BYOP presets now include OpenRouter, Groq, Together AI, DeepSeek, and Google Gemini (plus the existing xAI / OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama ones), so /provider add openrouter <key> works without a base URL. First launch also shows a Set up a provider choice (p) — pick a preset, paste a key, and Failure saves it and authenticates. Same wizard via /provider setup anytime.

item.title: New: structured GitHub PR tools (view / list / create / comment)
item.description: The agent can now run gh_pr_view, gh_pr_list, gh_pr_create, and gh_pr_comment as typed tools instead of hand-quoting gh through the terminal tool. Read-only view/list stay auto-approved like the equivalent shell commands; create and comment still prompt, since they change shared GitHub state. Requires the GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated.

item.title: Fix: BYOP models could silently overwrite each other in the /model picker
item.description: Merging every configured provider's model catalog into one list had a real bug: two providers exposing a model under the identical slug (e.g. both serving llama3) would silently overwrite one another — one provider's model just disappearing, not merely a display mix-up. Fixed the merge to keep both, and the /model picker now labels a model with its provider only when its name actually collides with another one, e.g. Llama 3 (ollama.example.com).

item.title: New: a structured git tool (status / diff / log / commit)
item.description: The agent can now run git_status, git_diff, git_log, and git_commit as typed tools instead of hand-quoting shell commands through the terminal tool. Read-only git commands stay auto-approved exactly like they already were for the terminal tool; git_commit still prompts like running git commit directly would.

item.title: Fix: browser tool permission bypass + redirect-based SSRF gap
item.description: A security review of the newly-added browser automation tools found that browser_get_text, browser_screenshot, and browser_close skipped the permission prompt entirely — once any navigation was approved once, the model could keep reading page content afterward with zero visibility. Fixed the permission mapping, and added a re-check after redirects/clicks so a navigation that lands on a blocked internal address (like a cloud metadata endpoint) can't have its content read afterward. Also added timeouts so a hung page can't wedge a tool call indefinitely.

item.title: New: /mcp-worker configure — save Cloudflare Worker credentials from inside a session
item.description: The stable workers.dev URL feature for remote MCP access previously required a separate failure mcp-worker configure CLI invocation. /mcp-worker configure <token> [worker-name] [account-id] does the same credential setup without leaving a running session.

item.title: New: browser tools are built in, plus /provider to add a custom model provider in-session
item.description: Browser automation (navigate, click, type, screenshot, read text) is now part of the default toolset, not just the separate browser-use persona. /provider add <name> <api-key> [base-url] adds a bring-your-own-provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, or any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint) without editing config.toml by hand.

item.title: New: browser automation — navigate, click, type, screenshot, read page text
item.description: A real headless Chrome/Chromium browser the agent can drive directly — no chromedriver or Playwright dependency. Every navigation is checked against private/internal addresses before the browser is ever allowed to load it.

item.title: New: merge every configured provider's models into one catalog, not just one
item.description: Failure Build now fetches x.ai's model list and every custom provider's own catalog on every launch, merging them into one list you can switch between with /model — instead of only refreshing whichever single provider happened to be active.

item.title: New: Dracula and Catppuccin Mocha themes
item.description: Two more built-in color themes, selectable the same way as every other theme in the TUI.

item.title: New: Android/Termux support — a native android-aarch64 build
item.description: Android has no general-purpose terminal, so there's no standalone .apk — instead this ships a native aarch64-linux-android build that runs inside Termux, which provides one plus a real Linux userland.

item.title: New: an original logo, and the auto-updater points at our own releases
item.description: Replaced the inherited braille-pattern logo with an original design, and pointed failure update at this repo's own GitHub Releases instead of upstream's.
